input:
MEDICAL REPORT:
This is the infant Belghit Abdelwahab aged 06 months from and living in Oran, from a non-consanguineous couple, former premature baby of 28 weeks, admitted to our level for the investigation of chronic respiratory distress since neonatal age. HISTORY:
Family:
Father: 37 years old, good health, smoker. Mother: 34 years old, good health. Siblings: only son
Atopy: no
Chronic respiratory disease in the family: no
Personal:
Physiological:
Pregnancy: well monitored
Delivery: at 28 weeks vaginally
Neonatal resuscitation: yes
Birth weight: 1kg400
Meconium: emitted<24h
No delay in umbilical cord fall
Breastfeeding: maternal then artificial from the age of 1 month. Vaccination: correct
Vitamin D received at the age of 1 month. Psychomotor development: judged good depending on age
Socio-economic level: good
Personal atopy: no
Pathological:
Extreme prematurity of 28 weeks, unexplained, with hospitalization from birth at the Neonat-CHU Oran department for severe respiratory distress having benefited from instillation of surfactant as well as CPAP (Medical report requested, not returned) placed on Flixotide upon discharge. History of the disease:
Since leaving the neonatal unit, the infant has had slight basithoracic drawing, according to the mother, without any worsening. 20 days before his admission to our level, a worsening of respiratory distress with the appearance of a cough, which motivated the parents to consult the EHS Canastel pediatric emergency department for the management of bronchiolitis. The infant stayed for 08 days at their level where he received: antibiotic therapy based on Amoxicillin 150mg/kg/d + programmed nebulization by asthalin, then transferred to our department for additional care on 04/12/2025
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 06kg800 (-1.12 DS) Height: 68cm (+0.62 DS)
General condition: preserved, T°: 37.8 c
Skin examination: good mucocutaneous coloring
ENT examination: Nasal obstruction, no cervical ADP, clean throat. Pleuropulmonary examination:
FR 54 c/min
	Signs of respiratory distress consisting of: susternal and basi-thoracic pulling
SPO2: 98% under 0.5l/min of O2
Lung auscultation: fine wheezing sounds in both lung fields
- Cardiovascular examination: HR: 110 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 audible, no murmurs or added noises
Digestive examination: soft abdomen, no organomegaly, accelerated transit: 04 bowel movements/day with fluid appearance without impact on hydration status. Urogenital examination: Male morphotype sex
BIOLOGY:
CBC: hyperleukocytosis at PNN GB: 22800; HB: 11.6 PLQT: 700,000
CRP: Positive at 27 VS accelerated: 28/62
Procalcitonin negative: <0.16
CREATININE: 4.28
Stool parasitology / stool culture: negative
RADIOLOGY:
Chest x-ray (07/12/2025): Chest distension with bilateral infiltrates
CAT:
Hospitalization. Conditioning. Infectious assessment requested
Monitoring: CHMD, blood sugar, temperature. Placement on antibiotic therapy based on amoxicillin + clavulanic acid with nebulization with asthalin. Preventive vitamin D supplementation. ECBC launched with inclusion in the Djazira studies. Evolution:
During his hospitalization which lasted 05 days
Clinic:
he presented several febrile peaks on D1 of his hospitalization, then apyrexia obtained on D3
Disappearance of respiratory distress on day 4 of hospitalization with successful oxygen weaning
Biology:
NFS check done before release: correct
CRP: 10 mg/l; procalcitonin: negative
Radiology: Given the situation (prematurity of 28 weeks with oxygen therapy for more than 28 days), bronchopulmonary dysplasia was suspected, hence the investigation of chronic respiratory distress in the infant
Cardiac ultrasound (10/12/2025): Unremarkable. Thoracic CT: Requested appointment on: 12/29/2025
Immunodeficiency assessment: requested
Given the clinical-biological stability, the infant was discharged on 10/12/2025 after receiving a dose of Palivizumab (synagis) with a follow-up appointment at the day hospital and an RDB at the specialist consultation.
CONCLUSION
This is the infant BELGHIT ABDELWAHAB aged 06 months, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous couple, with a personal history of prematurity of 28 weeks with neonatal respiratory distress, admitted to our level for the management of superinfected bronchiolitis on probable bronchopulmonary dysplasia. RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Retrieve the medical report from the CHU-Oran neonatal department
- Retrieve chest CT
- Retrieve the patient's immune deficiency assessment
RESIDENT: Dr TAMAZIRT ASSISTANT: Dr DOUKAR
